summary_text: Checklist (minimum evidence pack) for a short Xauto post comparing `日本制度` and an `海外制度` using primary sources only: 1. Scope lock: define which system in each country, comparison period, and what “制度” means (e.g., legal framework only, eligibility, funding, enforcement, outcomes). 2. Primary law set: collect and freeze direct legal texts for both sides (base law, amendments, core regulations, official gazette entries). 3. Official implementation sources: at least one implementing/ministerial directive or agency rulebook per side that explains operational details. 4. Latest official metrics: authoritative statistics/reports for each claim (e.g., agency annual reports, official dashboards, parliamentary/budget records) with publication date. 5. Terminology map: source-backed definitions for key terms so Japanese and overseas concepts are compared on equivalent constructs. 6. Evidence matrix: one row per claim with `claim -> source URL -> article/section -> exact quoted statement/date`. 7. Verification pass: each factual statement in draft has exactly one primary source citation and no secondary/media-only references. 8. Conflict check: flag any contradictory/updated rules (transitional provisions, regional variants) and capture the latest effective version. 9. Final preflight: confirm every citation is directly accessible (no mirrors, no third-party summaries) and includes `lang`, `title`, `publication`, and `retrieved_at` metadata. I verified no lingering kernel/bootstrap tool processes are running before switching tasks.
